channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: You can now fill your home with Ikea’s cheap and tiny new Bluetooth speaker
item.description: Alongside smart Lego bricks and lots of robots, one of the most anticipated gadgets announced at CES 2026 was a cheap Bluetooth speaker from the company best known for flat-packed furniture. Ikea is no stranger to electronics, but after ending its partnership with Sonos last year, its approach to Bluetooth speakers has been a focus […]

item.title: PlayStation Doesn’t Want to Fail the Same Way as Xbox
item.description: Sorry PC players, but Sony has more at stake than game sales outside of PlayStation 5.

item.title: Meilleur smartphone étanche : quel téléphone IP68 choisir en 2026 ?
item.description: Avis aux maladroites et maladroits : bienvenue sur notre sélection des meilleurs smartphones étanches, c'est-à-dire totalement résistants à l'eau (certification IP68), pour que les accidents aquatiques soient sans conséquences.

item.title: Match Group COO out, as dating apps struggle to connect with Gen Z
item.description: Match Group eliminates the COO role as CEO Spencer Rascoff has been managing operations.

item.title: Preorders for Apple’s colorful MacBook Neo come with a $25 gift card
item.description: The forthcoming MacBook Neo is certainly compelling — at least for the right person. One could argue that buying a last-gen MacBook Air is a better bet in most instances; however, if you’re still sold on picking Apple’s colorful budget laptop ahead of its launch on March 11th, Best Buy is throwing in a $25 […]

item.title: 'The precision and quality of the print finish are exceptional': Our 3D printing expert loved the beginner-friendly Anycubic Kobra S1 and it's $320 off right now
item.description: At under $430, the high-speed, beginner-friendly Anycubic Kobra S1 Combo is one of the best 3D printers we've ever used.

item.title: Exclusive: ‘I don’t think it would be good for Sigma to just chase a trend we don’t know could end in 6-12 months’ says CEO on its plans for compact cameras
item.description: We sat down with Sigma's CEO Kazuto Yamaki at the CP+ 2026 show in Japan to discuss the family-owned lens and camera maker's plans

item.title: The MacBook Neo might be a creators dream — hear me out
item.description: It’s not the most powerful Mac, but between iPhone Mirroring, macOS tools, and Apple’s ecosystem, the $599 MacBook Neo might be the perfect creative companion.

item.title: Oppo mise tout sur la Gen Z : ce que la marque prépare pour séduire les jeunes Français
item.description: En marge du MWC 2026, j'ai pu m'entretenir avec Elvis Zhou, PDG Oppo Europe, au sujet notamment du retour de la marque en France et de la stratégie qu'ils comptent adopter.

item.title: 26 blooming great Lego Botanicals deals for Mothers' Day — from colourful wildflowers to quirky cacti
item.description: A whole bouquet of bargains, with prices from £8.79.

item.title: Ce surpuissant laptop gaming sous RTX 5070 bénéficie d’une ristourne de plus de 700 €
item.description: Avis aux gamers, le HP Omen 16-ap0034nf, équipé d’une RTX 5070 et d’un écran 16" 2,5 K à 240 Hz, passe de 2 099 euros à 1 384 euros sur Cdiscount. Une belle occasion pour profiter d’une configuration performante à prix réduit.

item.title: Big savings for students and professionals — Microsoft’s Surface Laptop with Snapdragon X Plus, Copilot AI and 16GB RAM is £350 off
item.description: Save big on Microsoft’s 7th Edition Surface Laptop at Amazon, now down to £892 for a limited time.

item.title: Microsoft warns of new signed malware which deploys remote monitoring tools as backdoors
item.description: TrustConnect is back, this time used to deploy legitimate but weaponized RMM solutions.

item.title: The First Trailer for ‘The Testaments’ Takes Us Back to ‘Handmaid’s Tale’
item.description: 'One Battle After Another' star Chase Infiniti is bringing us back to the world of Gilead through fresh eyes this April.

item.title: This four-star 'polished GoPro alternative' from DJI just dropped to its lowest price since Black Friday
item.description: The four-star DJI Osmo Action 4 has dropped to £169 at Amazon — that's the lowest price we've seen since Black Friday

item.title: Airlines don’t have an AI problem. They have a foundational technology problem
item.description: Airlines won’t unlock AI’s value until they fix the technology foundations beneath it.

item.title: Nothing’s Phone 4a and Phone 4a Pro Strongly Reject Minimalism
item.description: You definitely won't mistake them for iPhones or Galaxy phones.

item.title: Zeno raises $25M to speed up production of its battery-swap motorbikes
item.description: The startup, co-founded by Tesla and Apple alumni, has sold nearly 1,000 of its motorbikes so far.

item.title: Les joueurs achètent moins de jeux sur Switch 2 et c’est encore la faute de la crise de la RAM
item.description: Nintendo peine à vendre beaucoup de jeux pour sa Switch 2. Le coupable est connu, la crise de la mémoire RAM. Ou plutôt ses conséquences sur un autre type de composant essentiel.

item.title: Le Nothing Phone (4a) Pro est le vrai successeur spirituel de OnePlus : nous l’avons pris en main
item.description: À peine deux jours après la présentation de l'iPhone 17e, Nothing dégaine son Phone (4a) Pro à Londres. Carl Pei ne cache même plus sa cible. C’est audacieux, un brin punk et surtout très bien orchestré pour voler la vedette au géant de Cupertino.

item.title: L’IA ne détruit pas l’emploi en Europe… pour l’instant, selon la BCE
item.description: Finalement, tout ne va pas si mal : selon une étude de la Banque centrale européenne, l'IA n'a pas provoqué la destruction du marché de l'emploi que beaucoup prophétisent. Mieux encore, la technologie créé de nouveaux emplois, mais les perspectives sur le plus long terme sont moins optimistes.

item.title: Prediction markets in the news are a dangerous gamble
item.description: Today on Decoder, let’s talk about prediction markets, which continue to insert themselves into the news cycle and the news itself in increasingly weird, unsettling, and potentially illegal ways. My guest today is Liz Lopatto, senior reporter at The Verge, who owns what we cheerfully call the chaos beat. Liz has been writing a lot […]

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Friday, March 6 (game #999)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Friday, March 6 (game #733)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Databricks built a RAG agent it says can handle every kind of enterprise search
item.description: Most enterprise RAG pipelines are optimized for one search behavior. They fail silently on the others. A model trained to synthesize cross-document reports handles constraint-driven entity search poorly. A model tuned for simple lookup tasks falls apart on multi-step reasoning over internal notes. Most teams find out when something breaks.Databricks set out to fix that with KARL, short for Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning. The company trained an agent across six distinct enterprise search behaviors simultaneously using a new reinforcement learning algorithm. The result, the company ...

item.title: Everything you need to know about streaming F1 on Apple TV
item.description: We’ve known Apple would follow up its blockbuster film F1: The Movie with live coverage of F1 races in 2026. Now that we’re approaching the first grand prix weekend of the year, the company has provided details on what fans can expect to see inside the Apple TV app and beyond. There’s already a dedicated F1 channel in the Apple TV app, which is where you’ll stream races live when the time comes. You can also watch practice sessions, sprint races and both pre- and post-race coverage. Apple offers a number of additional F1 videos there (I’d recommend watching the one on the new rules) and you’ll...

item.title: Nvidia pourrait ressusciter la RTX 3060 : le retour inattendu d’une carte graphique face à la crise
item.description: On la croyait définitivement rangée aux oubliettes de l'histoire informatique, mais la RTX 3060 préparerait son retour sur les étalages. Face à une pénurie de mémoire sans précédent vampirisée par l'intelligence artificielle, Nvidia s'apprêterait à relancer la production de sa carte graphique vieille de deux générations.

item.title: Test Be Quiet! Dark Perk Sym : une première souris convaincante pour le spécialiste du refroidissement
item.description: Après une incursion réussie sur le marché des claviers gamer, Be Quiet! s’attaque désormais au marché des souris avec la gamme Dark Perk, dont nous testons ici le modèle symétrique.

item.title: This Eye-Catching Solar EV Startup Just Got Closer to Hitting Production
item.description: Aptera says it finished building the first solar-powered car on its low-volume assembly line.

item.title: Première sortie des TV OLED LG G6 et Micro RGB, deux visions de l’image selon LG
item.description: Le téléviseur OLED LG G6 représente le haut de gamme de 2026 pour LG et l’offre LCD promet beaucoup notamment via plusieurs séries de TV Micro RGB (Mini LED RGB), des modèles que certains privilégiés ont pu voir à l’œuvre et en comparaison de la concurrence.

item.title: Vous avez -60 € sur la Garmin Vivoactive 6, une montre connectée lifestyle aux fonctions sportives poussées
item.description: La Garmin Vivoactive 6 est une montre connectée pensée pour le suivi sportif qui arbore un look lifestyle, ce qui fait que vous pouvez la porter dans n'importe quelle situation. Lancée à 329,99 euros, elle est à 269 euros sur Joybuy pour la version Pink Dawn.

item.title: Fuite de données chez Florajet : vos mots doux et messages d’amour sont peut-être sur le dark web
item.description: Les messages d’amour envoyés avec un bouquet de fleurs se retrouvent entre les mains des cybercriminels. Victime d’une cyberattaque, le géant de la livraison de fleurs Florajet a exposé plus d’un million de bons de commande. Ces données, parfois intimes, sont désormais sur le dark web.

item.title: Écran Amoled ou MIP transflectif : quelle est la meilleure technologie d’affichage pour les montres de sport
item.description: Sur le marché des montres de sport, deux technologies s'affrontent : Amoled d'un côté, MIP transflectif de l'autre. On fait le point sur les différences, avantages et défauts de chacune.

item.title: AI powers innovation – but it’s also powering the next wave of cyber attacks
item.description: With AI enabling fully automated cyber attacks, cyber resiliency has emerged as a critical strategy.

item.title: Best science-based weight loss program for long-term results
item.description: Start a Weight Watchers program for as low as $10/month for 10 months and get the first month free

item.title: 1 500 kW et charge gratuite : le plan colossal de BYD pour écraser le marché de l’électrique avec ses bornes de recharge
item.description: BYD vient de franchir un nouveau cap dans le domaine de la recharge pour voitures électriques. Le constructeur chinois a dévoilé une borne capable de délivrer une puissance de 1 500 kW par points de charge. De quoi recharger une voiture en 5 minutes à peine.

item.title: Florida woman given major jail sentence for illegally selling Microsoft product keys
item.description: Florida woman paid Texas company around $20 for Windows 10 licenses before selling the product keys illegally.

item.title: Meta will allow rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp in Europe, but for a fee
item.description: Meta will allow rival AI chatbot providers to offer their services on WhatsApp for a fee, which ranges from €0.0490 to €0.1323 per "non-template message," depending on the country.

item.title: Ikea’s Matter-compatible smart bulbs are now available in the US
item.description: After being announced last November, Ikea’s Matter-compatible smart home collection has been slowly trickling into its stores around the world over the past few months. Despite some early connectivity issues, one of the most anticipated items in that collection is Ikea’s new Matter-over-Thread smart bulbs. The company originally said they’d hit the US sometime in […]...

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg takes issue with the word ‘addictive’ in deposition shown at landmark trial
item.description: Jurors in the social media trial watched Zuckerberg’s deposition, which was recorded last year. Jurors in a bellwether trial about the impacts of social media on children watched a deposition of Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Wednesday that explored what the architects of Facebook and Instagram knew from internal research about the negative experiences of young users and how the company responded.New Mexico’s attorney general alleges that Meta violated state consumer protection laws in failing to disclose what it knew about the dangers of addiction to social media as well as child sexual exploita...

item.title: L’IA Gemini va permettre de retoucher précisément les images qu’elle génère
item.description: Retoucher une image, Gemini le fait déjà. Mais seulement sur les fichiers que vous téléverser vous-même dans l'application. Cela va bientôt changer.

item.title: Sans aucune pitié, Apex Legends lance une offensive radicale pour bannir définitivement les pires tricheurs
item.description: Apex Legends va muscler son système d'anti-triche pour bannir systématiquement les joueurs utilisant des appareils de type Cronus / XIM sur ses serveurs. Une initiative qui en suit de nombreuses autres dans l'industrie.

item.title: Apple Music mise sur la bonne volonté de l’industrie du disque pour signaler les chansons générées par IA
item.description: Apple Music fait appel à la bonne volonté de l'industrie du disque pour signaler la présence d'IA générative dans les œuvres musicales. Un système de tags a été mis en place par la plateforme, il est facultatif et Apple ne vérifie pas la présence effective d'IA dans les chansons.

item.title: It’s Time For Even More Outlandish ‘The Batman, Part II’ Casting Rumors
item.description: Plus, the shelved 'M3GAN' spinoff may finally see the light of day.

item.title: Apple Music adds optional labels for AI songs and visuals
item.description: Apple is asking artists and record labels on its music streaming platform to voluntarily label songs that were made using AI. The new "Transparency Tags" metadata system for Apple Music was announced in a newsletter to industry partners yesterday, according to Music Business Worldwide, and covers four categories, including track, composition, artwork, and music videos. […]...

item.title: Science Corp raises $230M as it races to bring its brain implant to market
item.description: A source close to the company says the round assigns Science a post-money valuation of $1.25 billion.

item.title: Still find parrying in games like Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 hard to time? Let me tell you about how a new controller’s fantastic speed and micro-switch triggers transformed my timing
item.description: I thought my aged reflexes were too slow for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 — then this controller with 3.5ms input latency and micro-switch dual-mode triggers changed everything.

item.title: Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man review — new Netflix movie manages to destroy the best British TV show of the 2010s in less than two hours
item.description: Peaky Blinders originally ended with a bang — now new Netflix movie The Immortal Man has given it the Game of Thrones curse.

item.title: ExpressVPN just gave AI agents the power to control your connection
item.description: ExpressVPN has launched a beta Model Context Protocol (MCP) server on desktop, becoming the first VPN service to let AI agents directly interact with its apps — all while promising the privacy and security VPN users expect.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra review: The stealth upgrade
item.description: You'd be forgiven for thinking that the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra looks a lot like the last four models. That's because it does, right down to its general design and rear camera layout. But on Samsung's latest flagship phone, some stealthy upgrades are hidden beneath its classic blocky silhouette that might go unnoticed by the casual observer. Those help make this year's release feel like a better deal than its most recent predecessor. It remains rather expensive, starting at the same $1,300 as before, but considering the price of RAM these days, that almost feels like a blessing. So while it w...
